arts.ttl: Leicester beat QPR and fog to reach fourth round
arts.sum: Watch the highlights as Leicester City beat Championship side QPR 6-2 in a foggy encounter to reach the FA Cup fourth round.

arts.ttl: Leicester City player ratings v QPR as trio stand out in emphatic victory
arts.sum: Two minutes later, Winks’ aimless ball into midfield was pounced upon, and a quick triangle of passes set up Jonathan Varane to rifle his first QPR goal into the bottom corner from 25 yards. All of the regulars played with James Justin at the double and with Stephy Mavididi, Facundo Buonanotte, Jamie Vardy also on the scoresheet before Wout Faes lashed a sublime sixth.

arts.ttl: Leicester run riot against QPR in thick fog at the King Power Stadium
arts.sum: The Foxes ended a five-match losing run in style.

arts.ttl: Manchester United and Leicester City Set Sights on Inter Milan’s Rising Star Francesco Pio Esposito
arts.sum: Francesco Pio Esposito, the youngest of the talented Esposito brothers, has been making waves in Italian football with his exceptional performances. The 19-year-old striker, currently on loan at Spezia from Inter Milan, has caught the attention of two prominent English clubs: Manchester United and Leicester City. The post Manchester United and Leicester City Set Sights on Inter Milan’s Rising Star Francesco Pio Esposito appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Justin scores for Leicester... but can you see it?
arts.sum: Watch as James Justin scores in the fog to give Leicester City an early lead against QPR in the FA Cup third round.

arts.ttl: Chelsea v Morecambe, Leicester v QPR, and more: FA Cup third round – clockwatch
arts.sum: Updates from the 3pm GMT kick-offs Live scoreboard | Get in touch with Tom Ilias Chair is the first QPR player to test Jakub Stolarczyk, who saves well, but the Leicester keeper cannot do anything about Jonathan Varane’s effort. QPR had turned up the heat in the previous five minutes or so and after Harry Winks failed to clear, Varane gathered the ball and slammed it low and into the bottom corner from all of 25 yards. Nottingham Forest: Carlos Miguel, Toffolo, Boly, Morato, Alex, Moreira, Ward-Prowse, Jota Silva, Yates, Sosa, Awoniyi. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Brentford and Leicester both make inquiry for winger – May need to move quickly as transfer rivals step ahead
arts.sum: Brentford and Leicester City have both shown an interest in Troyes youngster Cyriaque Irié and could provide competition to Lens for the 19-year-old. That’s according to Foot Mercato, who report that while the French side have been ‘seduced’ by the youngster, they may have to move ahead of Brentford and Leicester to get him. The The post Brentford and Leicester both make inquiry for winger – May need to move quickly as transfer rivals step ahead appeared first on Sport Witness.

arts.ttl: Leicester City team news v QPR as three changes made in strongest line-up
arts.sum: City (4-2-3-1): Stolarczyk; Justin, Coady, Faes, Kristiansen; Winks, Soumare; Buonanotte, El Khannouss, Mavididi; Vardy. Jannik Vestergaard limped off at Villa Park and is not in the squad against QPR, with Wout Faes handed his first start under van Nistelrooy.

arts.ttl: Leicester City vs Queens Park Rangers postponement latest as ice threatens FA Cup tie
arts.sum: Leicester City welcome Queens Park Rangers to the King Power Stadium on Saturday afternoon in the FA Cup but supporters will be keeping a close eye on the weather forecast amid fears of a possible postponement. According to the Met Office's weather forecast, temperatures will drop to as low as -3C overnight on Friday going into Saturday, which could lead to further treacherous conditions on the road.

arts.ttl: Leicester City first January transfer 'done' as negotiation details emerge
arts.sum: Leicester City are reportedly close to securing their first signing of the January transfer window in Parma's Woyo Coulibaly. The Foxes are yet to sign any reinforcements in the winter window despite Ruud van Nistelrooy admitting his squad is in need of new faces in their battle to stay in the Premier League.
